text: Robotic Diverting Colostomy Patient ML is a thirty eight year old female with grade four sacral decubitus ulcer. Past medical history is significant for encephalopathy and morbid obesity. Past surgical history is significant for an open cholecystectomy and an open appendectomy.

The surgical team employs a four trocar technique, inserting a 12mm trocar for the camera at the umbilicus, an 8mm trocar in the right upper quadrant, a 12mm trocar in the right lower quadrant for the stapler, and a 12mm trocar at the preoperatively marked ostomy site. An additional 5mm trocar is used to clear extensive omental adhesions obscuring the right side of the abdomen.
The robotic surgical system is docked over the patient's left lateral abdomen, positioning the robotic arms for the colectomy procedure.
The surgeon initiates lateral to medial mobilization of the sigmoid and descending colon, progressing the cephalad dissection towards the splenic flexure.

The surgeon ligates the inferior mesenteric artery using a vessel sealer, extending the ligation to the level of the rectum. The mobilization of the colon is visually confirmed before proceeding with the transection of the rectum.
The surgeon uses a grasper to secure the proximal colon through the twelve millimeter trocar site. A Proline suture is applied to mark the rectum, preparing for future colostomy reversal.
The surgeon exteriorizes the proximal colon through the twelve millimeter trocar site, followed by the maturation of the ostomy.
